query: dispatched a background worker into its own worktree, and after it claimed and closed its task, my main tracker had lost almost every other open issue
gave a parallel agent absolute file paths in its brief and its edits landed in the real checkout instead of the isolated copy it was supposed to work in
ran the tests inside an agent's isolated checkout but they kept exercising the main copy's code because the package was installed editable on the path
a freshly cut branch reported a clean rebase with nothing to do, then its first commit dragged in a pile of unrelated files from merges it had never actually caught up on
rescuing a crashed agent's partial work, rebasing its branch onto the latest tip threw away the files it had left sitting uncommitted on disk
once a couple of background agents finished, my merge and close commands quietly ran against a leftover agent checkout rather than the main directory
each agent in a parallel batch pushed its own branch on its own, and the shared task state came out garbled once the batch was done
merging a branch that had been cut off an older tip quietly flipped several already-finished tasks back to open, with no conflict ever shown to me
pulling at the end of a session flattened the merge bubbles I depend on to tell one task's integration from another
committing from inside an isolated checkout kept dumping a stray issues file at the repo root and scrambling the tracker's exported state
the close-time capture check stopped recognizing my longer task identifiers and skipped verifying capture for anything with a four-character id
closed a task using a shell variable for its id, the variable never expanded, and the capture gate just let the empty token sail through
upgrading the issue-tracker binary silently dropped our persistent memories out of its export, so we rolled the version back
every attempt to mine an existing repo's history aborted early, and it turned out a shelled-out subprocess was swallowing the input stream the loop was reading from
onboarding a brownfield repo, none of the reasoning the team argued out in old pull requests was anywhere in the project's memory
after installing a plugin the same hook fired twice on every command, and I wanted the project audit to catch the doubled registration
the pre-PR checklist told me to run Go test and a Go linter in a project that has no Go code in it whatsoever
I want a project to hard-stop an agent from running the wrong package manager, not just leave a note and hope it reads it
while a dispatched worker ran, my orchestrator just sat there blocked and idle until it returned instead of getting on with other work
two full test-suite runs ended up going at once in the same checkout and handed me a nonsense pass/fail tally with stray leftover processes still running
the documentation deploy job kept failing because a single dead cross-reference made the strict build bail out entirely
the docs site deployed green but half the pages were actually returning not-found, and nobody caught it until someone opened the live site
the landing-page cards rendered the raw emoji shortcodes as literal text instead of showing the icons
a correctness check we built only ran when somebody remembered to invoke it by hand, so the exact rot it was meant to prevent quietly crept back in
we kept adding reference pages but the navigation menu silently fell out of step with the pages that actually existed
a hook that worked fine from the repo broke the moment it was symlinked into the git hooks directory because it could no longer find its shared helper files
the guard that blocks edits right after a failed command kept tripping on unrelated later commands and wouldn't let me touch anything
my session-start priming never reached the dispatched sub-agents, so workers ran with none of the onboarding context the main session got
a skill that calls a helper by its repo-relative path just did nothing at all when run inside a different project, failing silently
a managed project kept inheriting the harness's noisy default behaviors instead of the quieter settings we standardize on
I ran the installer from inside a temporary checkout, and when that checkout was later cleaned up every installed link broke at once
every tiny edit to a stored note forces me to resend the whole note because there's no way to just tack on a line
our memory backend kept corrupting itself and stuffing a whole section with junk, so we decided to move off it onto something else
ever since we swapped the memory store, the assistant stopped surfacing older lessons and it feels like it forgets everything between sessions
our retrieval benchmark scored beautifully, but only because each test query basically already spelled out the exact note it was supposed to find
I keep bundling several small independent chores into one ticket instead of splitting them so they could be worked in parallel
